question.text: A project requires an initial investment of Rs. 1,00,000 and promises to return Rs. 40,000 per year for 3 years. At a discount rate of 10%, the Net Present Value (NPV) is closest to:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** NPV = -1,00,000 + 40,000/1.1 + 40,000/1.1^2 + 40,000/1.1^3 = -1,00,000 + 36,364 + 33,058 + 30,053 = -1,00,000 + 99,474 = -Rs. 526. Closest to Rs. -504. **The Trap:** Rs. 20,000 is the simple sum (40,000 × 3 - 1,00,000 = 20,000) without discounting. NPV accounts for time value. **Cross-Exam Context:** NPV is introduced in X-A and used extensively in X-B and XXI-B for investment evaluation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A negative NPV means the investment doesn't meet the required return threshold — look for alternatives with positive NPV.
